title: Diallyldithiocarbamidohydrazine (Dalzin) as an. Analytical Reagent. Part VI. Colorimetric Determination of Palladium
author: N. K. Dutt
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Dolzin has been used as a colorimetric reagent for palladium. The reagent is free from any absorption in the region 360-700 mix. The yellow complex has maximum absorption below 360 m µ , but all optical density measurements have been carried out at 365 m µ , Boer's law is obeyed within 1-20 ppm. of Pd. Interference by other ions has been avoided by using vereenate (EDTA) as a masking agent. The molar ratio method gives the ratio of palladium to dalzin as 1:1 in the coloured complex.

Interference by other ions has been avoided by using vereenate (EDTA) as a masking agent. The molar ratio method gives the ratio of palladium to dalzin as 1:1 in the coloured complex.
